page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8252C processor operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI for vector and neural network calculations. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory operations and data compression. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in Passmark memory scores and strong performance in large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth. Single-threaded gzip compression also registers in the top 25% tier. Most other compute, database, and web serving benchmarks, including Redis operations and static web serving, perform in the average tier. However, LLM text generation speeds for both small and medium models fall into the lower performance tier, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

The m5zn.12xlarge does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly efficient for network-heavy workloads and EBS-intensive operations. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for high-frequency compute tasks, network appliances, and memory-bound applications that require high single-core clock speeds and substantial network throughput without needing local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a high-performance, general-purpose server instance optimized for network and EBS throughput. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz, alongside 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. While it lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, it provides a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average performance in standard compute, database, and web serving tasks. This instance is well-suited for network-intensive applications, high-frequency compute workloads, and memory-bound tasks requiring high clock speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon CPU at 4.5 GHz, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency compute and network-optimized server featuring dedicated processors and high-speed memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
192 GB of DDR4 system memory with top-tier memory benchmark scores
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network throughput
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for high-frequency compute, network appliances, and database workloads
